latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director
investors@defidevcorp.co.uk
First Sentinel (Corporate Adviser)

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI develops and sells AI‑powered sales agents and advanced customer‑engagement tools for businesses. The flagship product, Eve, is an autonomous chatbot that learns from each interaction to improve lead conversion. The company also offers AI‑driven analytics and workflow automation for small‑to‑mid‑size enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The AI‑sales‑automation market is projected to exceed £10 billion by 2028, and Cykel AI’s solutions sit at the intersection of conversational AI and revenue‑operations, a niche where many incumbents lack deep data‑driven capabilities. Their technology gives customers a measurable lift in close rates and reduces sales cycle time, which keeps the business relevant as firms chase efficiency gains.
company_30s.current_state: Cykel AI is in a high‑cash‑burn phase. It has raised several rounds—most recently a £2.8 million equity raise in August 2025 and a £750 k placing in May 2025—to fund product development and go‑to‑market expansion. Revenue is still modest, but the company is actively scaling sales teams and building strategic partnerships. The shares have plunged dramatically, trading well below any historical valuation benchmarks.

rns_narrative: The company’s latest updates focus on funding and partnership milestones. In June 2025 it announced a new AI sales agent, Eve, and a partnership with Transpharmation that could unlock a new customer base. The August 2025 funding round and the 2025‑06 annual report confirm that Cykel AI is still raising capital and has a clear product roadmap, but it has yet to report any revenue growth.

A second theme is governance and structural change. The board reshuffle in November 2025 and the name change in January 2026 suggest a push to rebrand and streamline operations. These moves can signal a fresh start to investors who have been skeptical.

Finally, the company has embraced a bitcoin treasury reserve strategy, announced in May 2025. While this is a novel approach to balance‑sheet management, it signals a willingness to experiment with new financial structures to keep the business afloat.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent skepticism with pockets of opportunistic hope
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the deep crash as a buying window and are quietly stacking positions after each funding announcement, hoping the next partnership will ignite a surge.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The majority of chatter points to a lack of real revenue and the risk that the company may run out of cash. Critics argue that the product has not proven its market fit and that the bitcoin reserve is a gimmick rather than a safety net.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially a quiet, almost indifferent crowd, the tone has warmed slightly after each funding round, but the core fear—no proven sales pipeline—remains.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat and dead, meaning there is no mainstream hype. This silence is a green light for contrarians looking for a surprise play.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming earnings release in Q3 2026 could reveal any incremental revenue or a new partnership. Even a modest uptick in sales figures would be a strong signal.
If the company announces a new strategic alliance with a large enterprise vendor or a significant client win, that would validate the product and spark buying.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds 10‑fold the average daily volume—particularly on a support level near the recent low—would indicate fresh capital inflow and potential capitulation reversal.
Any regulatory approval or a major contract that unlocks a new market segment could act as a catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor daily volume spikes, the closing price relative to the 20‑day moving average, and any forward‑looking statements from the CEO about revenue targets. A breakout above the last support zone with volume > 50 % above the 20‑day average would confirm a mean‑reversion move.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation‑to‑recovery cycle. The APEX score is high, suggesting tight compression, and the price is still far below its 52‑week low. The next 2‑3 months should provide enough time for a potential bounce if a catalyst emerges.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (over 70 % down) sets up a classic mean‑reversion scenario. Historically, RED and GREEN signals—deep crashes—have delivered 39 % to 163 % average returns. If the stock recovers to 50 % of its pre‑crash peak, that is a 2‑to‑3× payoff on the current price. Given the high APEX score and dead market chatter, a sudden catalyst could trigger a rapid rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has not yet proven a repeatable revenue engine. Cash burn remains high, and the bitcoin treasury is a speculative hedge rather than a guaranteed cash source. If the next funding round fails or the partnership talks stall, the share could slide further into the 80‑90 % drop zone, potentially leaving investors with a near‑zero equity stake.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2.5× to 3× the current price if the share rebounds to halfway between the low and the last high. Potential downside: The stock could fall another 10‑15 % if the next round fails or the product fails to convert leads. The asymmetry is favorable because the loss is limited to a small percentage of the current price, while the upside is several times higher.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter a small position (e.g., 5‑10 % of available capital) after a confirmed volume spike that breaks the last support level. Place a stop just below the recent low to cap loss at 5‑7 %. Scale in with each subsequent bounce above the 20‑day moving average.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a Q3 earnings release that shows revenue growth or a partnership announcement. Alternatively, wait for a volume spike that exceeds 10× the 20‑day average and the price to close above the 50‑day moving average.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks, so this scenario is unlikely. The only terminal risk would be a prolonged failure to raise additional capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cykel AI’s deep crash and silent market create a contrarian play with a high upside if a funding or partnership catalyst sparks a rapid rebound, but the lack of proven revenue makes a cautious, small‑position approach the safest bet.
